---
title: "Create Follicle SData"
jupyter: xenium
params:
  sample: "default"
  path: "."
  cell_ids_file: "assets/stage_quality_area_all_rois.csv"
  radius: 250
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
execute:
  warning: false
---

```{python}
#| tags: [parameters]

sample = "default"
path = "."
cell_ids_file = "assets/stage_quality_area_all_rois.csv"
radius = 250
```

```{python}
import os
import pandas as pd
import spatialdata
import session_info
from timer import timer, timing_summary


def normalize_sample_id(value: str) -> str:
    return "".join(ch for ch in str(value).upper() if ch.isalnum())

with timer("Setup"):
    # 'path' is the staged sample-level zarr produced by create_sdata.
    zarr_path = path
    default_radius = float(radius)
```

## Load SpatialData

Reads the full-sample zarr store created by `create_sdata`.

```{python}
with timer("Read zarr"):
    sdata = spatialdata.read_zarr(zarr_path)
sdata
```

## Load cell IDs for this sample

Reads the cell ID reference file and filters to the cells annotated for this sample.
Each cell may have an optional per-cell radius; missing values fall back to the default radius.

```{python}
with timer("Load cell IDs"):
    _df = pd.read_csv(cell_ids_file)
    normalized_sample = normalize_sample_id(sample)
    _df["_normalized_roi"] = _df["Donor.ROI"].map(normalize_sample_id)
    matching_rois = _df.loc[_df["_normalized_roi"] == normalized_sample, "Donor.ROI"].drop_duplicates().tolist()
    if not matching_rois:
        raise ValueError(
            f"No follicle cells found for sample '{sample}'. "
            "Check naming in the samplesheet and cell_ids_file Donor.ROI column."
        )
    if len(matching_rois) > 1:
        raise ValueError(
            f"Sample '{sample}' matched multiple Donor.ROI values after normalization: {matching_rois}"
        )
    matched_roi = matching_rois[0]
    _df = _df.loc[_df["Donor.ROI"] == matched_roi].copy()
    if "radius" not in _df.columns:
        # no per-cell radius column — apply default to all cells
        _df["radius"] = default_radius
    else:
        # fill only missing values; explicit per-cell values are kept
        _df["radius"] = _df["radius"].fillna(default_radius)
    # keep all metadata columns so they can be embedded in each follicle zarr
    cells = _df.drop(columns=["Donor.ROI", "_normalized_roi"]).reset_index(drop=True)
print(f"{len(cells)} cell(s) found for sample '{sample}' (matched Donor.ROI='{matched_roi}'):")
print(cells[["cell_id", "radius"]].to_string(index=False))
```

## Subset FOV around each cell

For each cell, the centroid is read from the `cell_circles` element and transformed
into the `global` coordinate system. A square bounding box of ± radius (in global
units) is then used to spatially query the full-sample `SpatialData` object, and the
resulting subset is written to its own zarr store.

```{python}
import numpy as np
from spatialdata import transform
from spatialdata.transformations import get_transformation

with timer("Load cell circles"):
    # Transform circles into the global coordinate system so centroids and the
    # query bbox share units with the image element (pixel space for Xenium).
    circles = transform(sdata["cell_circles"], to_coordinate_system="global")
    # Map µm radius into global units using the same scale.
    affine = (
        get_transformation(sdata["cell_circles"], "global")
        .to_affine_matrix(input_axes=("x", "y"), output_axes=("x", "y"))
    )
    radius_scale = float(np.mean(np.abs([affine[0, 0], affine[1, 1]])))

os.makedirs("output", exist_ok=True)

for _, row in cells.iterrows():
    cell_id = row["cell_id"]
    radius  = float(row["radius"]) * radius_scale

    with timer(f"Subset {cell_id}"):
        # cell_circles stores pre-computed centroids as Point geometries
        if cell_id not in circles.index:
            print(f"  WARNING: {cell_id} not found in cell_circles — skipping")
            continue
        centroid = circles.loc[cell_id, "geometry"]
        cx, cy   = centroid.x, centroid.y
        min_coordinate = [cx - radius, cy - radius]
        max_coordinate = [cx + radius, cy + radius]

        sdata_fov = sdata.query.bounding_box(
            axes=("x", "y"),
            min_coordinate=min_coordinate,
            max_coordinate=max_coordinate,
            target_coordinate_system="global",
        )

    with timer(f"Write {cell_id}"):
        out = os.path.join("output", f"{cell_id}.zarr")
        # embed cell-level metadata as obs columns on the target cell
        if "table" in sdata_fov.tables:
            obs = sdata_fov["table"].obs
            obs["follicle_id"] = cell_id
            if cell_id not in obs.index:
                print(f"  WARNING: {cell_id} not in table.obs — per-cell metadata not embedded")
            else:
                meta = row.drop(labels=["cell_id", "radius"]).to_dict()
                for col, val in meta.items():
                    obs.loc[cell_id, col] = val
        sdata_fov.write(out, overwrite=True)

    print(f"  {cell_id}: centroid=({cx:.1f}, {cy:.1f})  radius={radius}µm  →  {out}")
```

## Summary

```{python}
with timer("Summary"):
    print(f"Sample:        {sample}")
    print(f"Input zarr:    {zarr_path}")
    print(f"Cells written: {len(cells)}")
    for _, row in cells.iterrows():
        print(f"  output/{row['cell_id']}.zarr  (radius={row['radius']}µm)")
```

## Timings

```{python}
timing_summary()
```

## Session Info

```{python}
session_info.show()
```
